data.title: Hensoldt Lifts Guidance for Key Orders Measure as Germany Ramps Up Defense Spending
data.description:
By Cristina Gallardo

German defense company Hensoldt lifted its full-year guidance for a key orders indicator, citing an increase in demand from its home country.

The sensor manufacturer said the German government has ramped up procurement of many types of defense equipment, leading to a higher number of concrete orders.

Germany increased its military spending by 28% last year, reaching $88.5 billion, becoming the largest defense spender in Europe since the country's reunification in 1990, according to the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute.

The rapid expansion of the German defense budget comes in response to worsening relations between Europe and Russia, following Russia's large-scale invasion of Ukraine. However, German defense makers had flagged a delay between the increase in spending and orders coming in.

Hensoldt said it now expects its book-to-bill ratio, which compares orders to sales, of 1.6 to 1.9 this year, up from a previous estimate of around 1.2. It also nudged up its adjusted Ebitda margin guidance to 18% or higher from previous expectations of approximately 18%.

Revenue should come in at around 2.5 billion euros ($2.9 billion) for the year, the company said, narrowing a prior forecast of between 2.5 billion and 2.6 billion euros. Jefferies analysts said Hensoldt's transition to a new logistics center could explain revenue now being forecast at the lower end of the range.

Hensoldt also backed its medium-term guidance and 2030 revenue goal.

The company, whose portfolio includes radars for Eurofighter jets and optronics for the Leopard 2 tanks, said it was expanding its manufacturing capacity to cope with the increase in orders, via automation of processes, a new logistics center and a new building in Oberkochen, south of Germany.

"The fact that Germany is investing strongly in its own security due to the current security situation and that this political will is now being reflected in concrete orders is an important step towards genuine defense capability," Chief Executive Oliver Dorre said.

Analysts had expected a slight guidance update at the company's capital markets day on Nov. 11. Hensoldt is scheduled to post third-quarter results on Nov. 7.

Write to Cristina Gallardo at cristina.gallardo@wsj.com

data.title: Alten rises as Q3 results ease concerns despite auto sector drag
data.description:
** Shares in French software engineering group Alten climb more than 5% as Q3 results give reassurance over its activity

** The company reported a 1.3% revenue decline due to automotive sector exposure but confirmed its full-year outlook

** TP ICAP Midcap notes 2026 will benefit from "a more normal schedule" and easier comparisons

** Brokerage says Alten's specialized business model, focused on R&D services, project management, and IT architecture, provides resilience amid macro uncertainties and is less vulnerable to GenAI disruption than software support roles

** Shares are on track for best day since early March if gains hold

** Shares sit among best performers on France's SBF 120 index

data.title: SHARP unveils the new "LDK+" EV concept model at Japan Mobility Show 2025
data.description:
TOKYO, Oct 24, 2025 - (JCN Newswire) - Sharp has developed the second iteration of its EV (electric vehicle) concept model "LDK+," focusing on the parking time and proposing its use as an "extension of the living room." Under the new key message "Part of your home," the model will make its debut at Japan Mobility Show 2025, one of Japan's largest mobility events, held at TOKYO BIG SIGHT (Koto-ku, Tokyo) from October 30 (Thursday) to November 9 (Sunday) this year.

In September 2024, Sharp unveiled the first "LDK+" concept model. By integrating Sharp's proprietary AI technology "CE-LLM" (*1), along with AIoT and sensing technologies, Sharp proposes a new future for mobility that enables comfortable and sustainable lifestyles by connecting EVs with living spaces and energy devices.

The second iteration of "LDK+" retains the original concept while featuring both high maneuverability with its compact body and a spacious, relaxing interior. Developed based on the "Model A" EV by Hon Hai Technology Group (Foxconn). (*2), this compact minivan model offers an expansive cabin layout.

When parked, the vehicle can be used as a theater room or a remote workspace. A console box equipped with a table and projector is placed between the driver's and passenger's seats. By swiveling the driver's seat to face backward, it creates a living room-like atmosphere where you can sit around with the rear seats. Pulling down the screen installed above the rear seats allows you to enjoy movies or conduct online meetings on a large display. Through Sharp's AIoT platform, which connects AI and home appliances, the vehicle links with household devices such as kitchen appliances, air conditioning, and laundry systems. The AI learns residents' lifestyles and preferences, creating personalized new ways of living. In addition, the system can connect with V2H (Vehicle to Home) solutions, enabling efficient energy management by integrating solar power generation and residential storage batteries.

Sharp is committed to creating new values through the fusion of future lifestyles and mobility.

*1 CE-LLM (Communication Edge - Large Language Model) is Sharp's proprietary AI technology. By equipping edge devices with AI capabilities and seamlessly leveraging cloud AI when needed, it enables smooth and comfortable communication. CE-LLM brings AI closer to everyday life, creating a world where AI naturally integrates into both daily living and business settings.

*2 Headquarters: New Taipei City, Taiwan, Chairman: Young Liu

- Some of the functions and devices described in the text are not included in the concept models exhibited this time, and some are exhibited as mockups.

- "LDK+" and "CE-LLM" are registered trademarks of Sharp Corporation.

- AIoT is a coined word created by combining AI (artificial intelligence) and IoT (Internet of Things), and is a registered trademark of Sharp Corporation.

Source: Sharp Corporation

Copyright 2025 JCN Newswire . All rights reserved.

data.title: EU sanctions hit Russian energy majors, Chinese buyers
data.description:
The EU has sanctioned China’s state-backed trading arm and two major refineries as part of a broad 19th Russian sanctions package, which mounts pressure on the Kremlin’s foreign trade partners and bans transactions with energy majors Rosneft and Gazprom Neft, as per Chemweek.

A statement on X from European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen confirmed that EU member states had approved the bloc’s 19th sanctions package against Russia, after a unanimous vote on new measures designed to squeeze the Kremlin’s oil and gas revenues.

A further Oct. 23 statement from the commission outlined sweeping new measures clamping down on major Russian oil traders, including Litasco Middle East DMCC, a subsidiary of state-owned energy company Lukoil, and Chinaoil, the trading arm of PetroChina. The parent company is China’s largest energy firm and marks the first major state-backed Chinese entity to be targeted by EU sanctions.

New measures also sanctioned two of China's state-owned refineries, which are also major producers of petrochemicals: Yulong Petrochemical Co. and Liaoyang Petrochemical Co. They identify the companies as major consumers of Russian oil. The 400,000-b/d Yulong, which was also recently sanctioned by the UK, sources millions of barrels of Russia's Urals crude through the Eastern Siberia-Pacific Ocean oil pipeline, and PetroChina's Liaoyang subsidiary is the only Chinese refinery running exclusively on Russian oil, the document said.

The full sanctions package designates 69 individual entities, including three in India and 12 in China. Additionally, the bloc listed a Tartarstani conglomerate active in the oil sector, five Russian banks and one crypto exchange.

The commission confirmed a decision to impose a full transaction ban on Russia's state-owned oil companies Rosneft and Gazprom Neft, which were previously afforded energy-related exemptions. The move follows similar steps from the UK and US authorities, which recently sanctioned Rosneft and Lukoil, another energy heavyweight.

Rosneft and Gazprom Neft were already subject to partial restrictions, including sanctions on Rosneft’s shipping arm and the Vadinar refinery in India, but had so far avoided blanket transaction bans. The two companies are together responsible for supporting 2 million b/d of oil from Russian ports, according to S&P Global Commodities at Sea data.

Gazprom, Gazprom Neft and Rosneft own 20 Russian refineries in total with a combined crude processing capacity of about to 3 million b/d, although intensive Ukrainian drone attacks have recently hit operations across the country.The transaction ban may further complicate BP’s already stalled effort to sell its 19.75% stake in Rosneft, which has been subject to a Russian government veto. BP will also be unable to access dividends in the interim. BP declined to comment when contacted by Platts.

Rosneft Deutschland also still holds stakes in three German refineries under government trusteeship since 2022. It was last instructed to sell the assets before a deadline of March 10, 2026. UK sanctions provided an exemption for Rosneft's German subsidiary to preserve the country's refining operations.“While it is early days in the same way that the UK has provided exemptions, I expect the EU and US to have either designed their sanctions, or put in place appropriate permissions, to allow the Rosneft entities under German trusteeship to continue operating," said Mark Hunting, a partner at energy law firm Bracewell.A US announcement designating the two Russian energy companies on Oct. 22 triggered a rally in December Brent crude oil futures, which jumped to $65 per barrel during Asian trading hours after a week of languishing around five-month lows.Rosneft and Lukoil have not publicly responded to the US sanctions, which give companies until Nov. 21 to wind down operations. Lukoil postponed a board meeting scheduled to discuss dividends on Oct. 23, citing “the current circumstances.” The Russian government also has yet to comment.In an effort to combat circumvention of existing measures, the commission also designated 117 new shadow tankers used to sell Russian oil above its price cap and maritime registries providing false flags. New listings add to an existing blacklist of 442 vessels from previous EU enforcement efforts.

“This is a major blow to Russia’s war machine,” Dan Jorgensen, the EU’s commissioner for energy, said in a statement.New regulations shift the focus to the Chinese refining sector after months of scrutiny of the Indian market by the US and Europe under its 18th sanctions package. Market sources call the new measures a major escalation from EU authorities but express uncertainty over future trade repercussions.A Beijing-based energy expert, who was not permitted to comment publicly, said the sanctions could be unlikely to disrupt pipeline supplies delivered under government-to-government arrangements, despite new restrictions on Rosneft and its buyers. Traders agreed that pipeline flows could prove difficult to monitor for EU officials.

Responding to the announcement, a spokesman with China's Ministry of Foreign Affairs said, "China expresses strong dissatisfaction with this and firmly opposes it, having already made stern representations to the European side." Petrochina was not immediately available for comment. Meanwhile, EU ministers said the bloc is already working on its next round of sanctions. In an Oct. 21 speech at the EU's Foreign Affairs Council, commission vice president Kaja Kallas said lawmakers were discussing additional measures, including a tougher stance on shadow tankers and additional restrictions targeting third countries.

Future measures may include stronger permissions for authorities to board suspected shadow tanker vessels, as well as a more robust EU-wide approach to combat the growing shipping network, she said. "After the 19th package, we should also work on the next package," she said.

mrchub.com

data.title: UK inflation expectations rise to highest since April, Citi/YouGov survey shows
data.description:
The British public's expectations for inflation over the next 12 months rose to 4.2% in October, the highest since April, a monthly survey by YouGov for U.S. bank Citi showed on Friday.

Citi said the reading was likely to reinforce the wariness some members of the Bank of England's Monetary Policy Committee feel about cutting interest rates further, even after September inflation came in below the BoE's 4% forecast.

Financial markets moved rapidly to price in a nearly 80% chance of a further quarter-point rate cut this year after weak labour market data last week was followed by lower-than-expected headline inflation.

Previously, the next BoE rate cut had not been expected until March or April next year after August's rate cut was only approved by a narrow 5-4 margin due to some policymakers' fears that inflation was becoming too persistent.

"We think inflation expectations remain an important aspect of the monetary policy framework, particularly for those who are cautious about further cuts," Citi economists Callum McLaren-Stewart and Michel Nies wrote.

"For those who favour a hold, a further drift in expectations may be at least as significant as an undershoot in realised CPI," they added.

Year-ahead inflation expectations had stayed in a narrow range of 3.9% to 4.0% for the previous five months on Citi's measure.

Long-term inflation expectations also rose to 4.2% in October from 4.1% in September, Citi said.

The results were based on a YouGov survey of 2,005 adults conducted on October 21 and October 22.

data.title: Saab's Pipeline Remains Strong Despite Third-Quarter Order Miss — Market Talk
data.description: Saab's order intake was slightly lower than expected in the third quarter, but the pipeline of larger orders remains very strong, Kepler Cheuvreux analyst Aymeric Poulain says. Sales and EBIT are in line with forecasts, while the raised organic growth range to 20%-24% is also in line with the bank's growth assumptions of 22%. "We assess that Saab has the strongest product portfolio in the European defense industry and offers very good growth prospects, with potential order flows pointing to a two- to three-fold increase in sales and margins above 12% by 2030." However, it says the share has already largely priced in this growth. Kepler Cheuvreux reiterates its reduce rating and 350 kronor target price on the stock. Shares rise 4.6% to 515 kronor. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

data.title: Shanghai copper rises on China plenum boost, Trump-Xi meeting confirmation
data.description:
Shanghai copper spiked on Friday after the White House confirmed a meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping, and as China's high-stakes plenum vowed to boost consumption and technological innovation.

The most-traded copper contract on Shanghai Futures Exchange surged 2.50% to 87,720 yuan ($12,315.04) per metric ton at the close of daytime trade, ending the week up by 2.59%.

The benchmark three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange was up 0.67% to $10,927 a ton as of 0733 GMT, set to end the week up by 3.04%.

China's Communist Party concluded its Fourth Plenum on Thursday, vowing to focus on technological innovation and bolster consumption. The communique that outlined China’s priorities for the next five years was in line with market expectations.

China will increase the percentage of household consumption of gross domestic product significantly over the next five years, officials told reporters at a news conference addressing the outcome of the plenum said on Friday.

A detailed five-year plan will be released in March.

Meanwhile, White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt confirmed the Trump-Xi meeting for next Thursday in South Korea as part of the U.S. President's trip to Asia. Trump expressed optimism about a trade deal with China later, saying "everyone’s going to be very happy".

The meeting was once called into question after Trump threatened to cancel it following China's expansion of export curbs on rare earth minerals earlier this month.

Elsewhere among SHFE base metals, aluminium gained 0.45%, zinc added 0.77%, lead rose 0.94%, nickel increased 0.81%, and tin was up 1.19%.

Among other LME metals, aluminium gained 0.42%, zinc was up 0.56%, lead added 0.42%, tin ticked up 0.30%, and nickel posted sole drop at 0.25%.

($1 = 7.1230 Chinese yuan renminbi)

data.title: Czech Republic Consumer Morale Hits Over 5-Year High
data.description:
The consumer confidence indicator in the Czech Republic climbed to 107.4 in October 2025 from 103.5 in the previous month.

This marked the highest reading since February 2020, driven by a sharp decline in the share of consumers expecting the country’s overall economic situation to deteriorate.

At the same time, the share of households expecting an improvement in their financial situation over the next year increased.

Meanwhile, a slightly higher number of respondents viewed their current financial situation as worse than a year earlier, while the share of those who do not plan to make major purchases in the coming twelve months also edged up.

data.title: FTSE 100 Steadies at Record High
data.description:
The FTSE 100 was little changed on Friday after reaching a record high in the previous session, though it remains up about 2.5% for the week.

The index was supported by upbeat UK economic data, with September retail sales adding to optimism after stronger public finance figures and softer inflation, suggesting consumers remain resilient.

NatWest shares jumped nearly 4% after the bank reported a profit beat, upgraded its 2025 income and returns guidance, and highlighted solid momentum across its business lines.

Its update echoed strength seen across the banking sector, which continues to benefit from higher-for-longer interest rates.

LSE shares rose over 4%, building on a 6.7% surge the day before following robust results.

In contrast, GSK slipped more than 1% despite US approval of its blood cancer drug Blenrep, as analysts cautioned over regulatory nuances.

Precious metal miners Fresnillo and Endeavour declined 2.5% and 0.9%, respectively, as gold prices retreated from record highs.

data.title: Rentokil Initial's Update Points to Good Progress — Market Talk
data.description: Pest-control provider Rentokil Initial's third quarter helps to build conviction that North America's organic growth can rehabilitate toward 5%, Bernstein analysts Will Kirkness and Filippo Giardini say. North America pest control's key line is services revenue, which grew organically by 1.8%, the analysts say. Within that, although non-contract, short-term jobs growth was above trend and contract growth was below, the net gain in contract portfolio was the best it has been in a while, the analysts say. Contract portfolio refers to retention, price and new contract sales. "Pricing is above inflation so that implies that volumes are still negative but the direction of travel sounds good," Bernstein says. Shares are down 0.1% at 440.60 pence and have risen 18% over the past 12 months. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

data.title: German Services Sector Rises More than Anticipated
data.description:
The HCOB Germany Services PMI rose to 54.5 in October 2025, up from 51.5 in September and well above market expectations of 51, according to the flash estimate.

This marked the second consecutive month of expansion and the strongest reading since May 2023, driven by a higher volume of new business.

In response, companies resumed hiring activity after two consecutive months of job declines.

data.title: SPX: S&P 500 Climbs 0.6% as Emboldened Traders Look Past Worry, Bet on Risk Plays
data.description:
Key points:
  • S&P 500 posts modest gain
  • Earnings power momentum
  • Govt blackout locks up data

Corporate America is churning out strong earnings data and that’s leading investors to believe that, maybe, it’s the right time to be invested.

💪 Earnings Strength Powers Risk Appetite

  • The S&P 500 climbed 0.6% Thursday, bringing its weekly gain to 1.1% and leaving it within striking distance of a fresh record.
  • Investors found comfort in better-than-expected corporate results as US companies remain resilient despite trade jitters, bad-loan concerns, and a government shutdown that’s about to hit its fourth week.
  • The absence of key economic data — normally a compass for traders — has put earnings front and center. With profits beating expectations across sectors, traders are rotating back into riskier plays and brushing off geopolitical noise.

🏛️ Indexes Near Records

  • The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 144 points, or 0.3%, while the Nasdaq Composite advanced 0.9% as tech and consumer stocks regained their footing.
  • All three indexes yesterday floated near all-time highs before paring gains in the final hour of trading.
  • Maybe that’s what happens when your best macro indicator is an earnings report from Coca-Cola.

📊 Shutdown Delays Data

  • With the government shutdown still very much a thing, the steady drip of economic data has turned into a total drought. That’s making corporate earnings reports a proxy for real-time economic health.
  • In a tiny sign of progress, investors are eagerly awaiting the September consumer price index, now scheduled for release Friday after the administration recalled select workers to finish compiling the report.
  • The CPI will also help determine the path for Fed policy. Traders have slightly dialed back expectations for aggressive rate cuts, with odds of a half-point of easing through December slipping from 95.5% to 91.9%, per CME’s FedWatch Tool.

data.title: Accor jumps as FY targets hike, extra buyback overshadow slight Q3 miss
data.description:
** Shares in Accor rise almost 6% to their highest since late August, after Europe's biggest hotel group "reassured" with its Q3 figures set

** While the firm narrowly missed market expectations on hard revenues, J.P. Morgan calls the results "very reassuring", pointing out the upgraded core profit target and additional buyback plans

** Accor plans to launch the extra 100 million euro buyback in Q4, and aims to grow its annual EBITDA by 11%-12% on a constant currency basis versus 9%-10% guided previously

** Jefferies echoes JPM's sentiment, adding that comments on potential listing of Accor's hotel and restaurant brand Ennismore, and ongoing disposal process of Essendi "should be definitely taken well" and support shares

** Up to the previous session's close, shares were down 10.3%​ YTD

** If gains hold, the stock will see its best day since late June

data.title: Hexagon up after margin beat, cost cuts plan
data.description:
** Shares in Hexagon rise around 5% after the Swedish industrial technology group posted a better-than-expected Q3 margin and announced a cost savings initiative

** Q3 EBIT margin came in at 26.8% versus a consensus estimate of 26.5% cited by J.P.Morgan, which the broker says resulted in a slight headline adjusted EBIT beat of around 1%

** The group said it launched a cost improvement program targeting EUR 110 million in annual savings, starting in Q4 and reaching full effect by end of 2026

** It also took a EUR 186 million impairment charge on R&D investments

** "Key from here will be the extent to which investors can underpin this as a final clearing of the decks or whether further cost savings, impairments, divisional changes etc. are required," JPM says

** The stock is on track for its best day since early September, if gains hold

data.title: U.S. Futures, European Stocks Edge Up
data.description:
U.S. futures for the S&P 500 were up 0.2% and futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed 0.1%. Changes in futures do not necessarily predict movements after the opening bell.

In Europe, the Stoxx Europe 600 gained 0.3% in morning trading. Lifco Series B rose 10% and Trelleborg Series B rose 7%. On the other hand, HIAB dropped 9.9%, and Signify slipped 7.4%. The FTSE 100 added 0.1%. Other stocks in Europe were up as France's CAC 40 increased 0.2% and Germany's DAX rose 0.2%.

The Wall Street Journal Dollar Index was flat at 96.28.

In commodities, Brent crude dropped 0.5% to $65.64 a barrel, and WTI crude fell 0.4% to $61.52 a barrel. The European benchmark price for natural gas, the Dutch futures contract TTF, was down 1% to 32.14 euros a megawatt hour.

The German 10-year Bund yield was flat at 2.589%, and the yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury held steady at 4.007%. Bond prices and yields move in opposite directions.

Stocks in Asia were up as Japan's Nikkei 225 index climbed 1.4%, and Hong Kong's Hang Seng climbed 0.7%. China's benchmark Shanghai Composite climbed 0.7%.

-This article was automatically generated by MarketWatch.

Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: Signify Needs Strong Actions to Boost Business — Market Talk
data.description:
0749 GMT - Signify needs to take more decisive measures to strengthen its business profile, ING analyst Marc Hesselink writes in a note. The Dutch lighting company lowered its full-year guidance amid price pressure in Europe and a slower U.S. market. The company is facing increasingly challenging conditions, he says. While free cash flow remains solid, it is largely consumed by leases, dividends, and share buybacks, adds. Shares are down 6.7% at 21.72 euros. ( najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0718 GMT - Gotion's decision to scrap its Michigan battery-plant plan is not surprising, Morningstar analyst Vincent Sun says. State of Michigan said that a unit of Chinese battery company Gotion High-Tech has abandoned a plan to build a battery factory in the state. Congressman John Moolenaar said the state' decision means "common sense wins" as local residents "do not support a company beholden to the Chinese Communist Party setting up shop in their town." Given the current U.S.-China tensions, it's highly unlikely the project will get green light from either side, Sun says. "It's getting harder with China's export control measures," he notes. Given CATL faced U.S. political pushback on building a joint venture with Ford in the U.S., it's less likely that Gotion manufactures directly in the U.S., he adds.(sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0633 GMT - Kering's recovery needs better prospects in the key Chinese market, Berenberg analysts write in a research note. "Under new CEO Luca de Meo, Kering is rapidly becoming a better business," the analysts say. The outlook for the French luxury group, however, is highly geared toward both the Chinese and less wealthy consumer segments, to which it is highly exposed, Berenberg says. While improving, trends in China remain weak, they say. Furthermore, the response to the new Gucci offer was described by the group as pretty good, but not at the same level as seen in the U.S., the analysts add. Berenberg reiterates its sell rating on the stock. Shares closed at 344.95 euros on Thursday. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

data.title: OPAL to issue 5-year bonds, bankers say
data.description:
India's ONGC Petro Additions Ltd (OPAL) is planning to raise 10 billion rupees ($114 million), including 8 billion rupees of a greenshoe option, through sale of bonds maturing in five years, three merchant bankers said on Friday.

OPAL, in which ONGC owns more than 95%, has invited coupon and commitment bids for the issue on Tuesday, they said.

Earlier this month, Reuters reported that ONGC provided a corporate guarantee to OPAL's bonds, which is its first such guarantee to a rupee-denominated debt of a subsidiary in 15 years, to raise its credit appeal.

Currently, OPAL has outstanding bonds worth 17.60 billion rupees. It had last tapped the bond market 15 months ago and raised 1.60 billion rupees through a three-year bond issue at 8.39%.

The company did not reply to a Reuters email seeking comment.

data.title: French Manufacturing Sector Remains Fragile in October
data.description:
The HCOB Flash France Manufacturing PMI rose slightly to 48.3 in October 2025 from 48.2 in September, in line with market expectations, according to preliminary estimates.

Although this marks a two-month high, the figure still points to contraction, signaling sharper declines in production and new orders.

Sluggish client demand and ongoing domestic political uncertainty, together with weak global conditions, continued to weigh on exports.

In response, manufacturers cut output prices for a second consecutive month to stimulate demand, while input cost inflation eased to its slowest pace in nearly five years as energy and material prices stabilized.

Employment grew modestly, mainly through temporary hires, reflecting cautious workforce planning.

Business confidence slipped to its lowest since mid-year, with many firms expecting further production declines amid political instability and soft external demand.

data.title: Stressed market conditions in derivatives on HIAB (259/25)
data.description:
NASDAQ NordicProduct information

Stressed market conditions in derivatives on HIAB (259/25)

Nasdaq has decided to call for stressed market conditions in derivatives onHiab Oyj (HIAB) for four hours, after which time an assessment of marketconditions will be made to determine if stressed market conditions are stillvalid.

Stressed market conditions in derivatives are called for when the underlyingasset to a derivative experience high and short term intraday volatility orwhen other factors causes high uncertainty in the pricing of derivatives. Whenstressed market is called for it means that market makers are allowed to quoteprices with double the normal spread.

For contact details please see attached file.https://view.news.eu.nasdaq.com/view?id=b881486cd07125769cd22a7564c663012&lang=en&src=omxlink

data.title: German Manufacturing Remains in Contraction
data.description:
The HCOB Germany Manufacturing PMI ticked up to 49.6 in October 2025, compared to market expectations and September’s 49.5, signaling a continued mild contraction in the sector.

Output expanded for the eighth consecutive month, though at a slower pace, with the output index easing to a three-month low.

There was a marginal rise in new orders after the previous month’s dip, while backlogs continued to decline.

Employment fell for the seventeenth straight month, the sharpest drop since June, as manufacturers remained cautious amid supply chain challenges, particularly in semiconductors and machinery.

On prices, input costs continued to fall, albeit at a slower rate, while output prices increased for only the second time in two and a half years.

Business confidence weakened further, reflecting concerns over high costs, global competition, and persistent uncertainty in the industrial outlook.
